    1. The Shadow Self
</h2>

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h3>"It's never too late to have a happy childhood." Tom Robbins.</h3><p>Right out of the gate I wanna say this:</p><p>I roll my eyes at anything and everything that's described as a "personality test". </p><p>I hate the idea of people putting me into a box. And I'm guessing you do too?</p><p>But if you're looking for answers in your life right now... </p><p>Holy shit did I discover a new way of using those silly tests that might blow your mind today. </p><p>Let me explain how this landed on my lap.</p><p>One night at a group dinner in LA, I met a fun but intense girl obsessed with the Meyers-Briggs personality test. She wanted to share her 'letters' with everyone. And she expected everyone to know theirs. (In California, many 'new culture' corporations force their employees to take this test.)</p><p>We got along, and the tequila was good, so later, when we were chillin' she convinced me to take the test online. For shits and giggles. Ok. No harm in that.</p><p>One of the letters that came back in the results: 'I'. 'I' stands for introvert.</p><p>Oooh, she hated that. For her, it didn't make sense with how we met. My personality was "too fearless, too confident, and too... social". </p><p>She couldn't let it go. She was convinced that "I did it wrong." Or that "I was dishonest with my answers". Watching her rationalize the results was painful.</p><p>Luckily, I salvaged the night and avoided a continuing debate by switching the conversation back to her... And HER wonderful letters.</p><p>But for the next few days, it haunted me. This whole "personality test" thing...</p><p>Why?</p><p>Because TBH it was spot on. I knew it was telling the truth. I AM an introvert.</p><p>Even though in many social situations, I project something way different. Somehow that online test got it right.</p><p>But ok. So what right?</p><p>It's just another system. Like astrology. Like palm reading. Another excuse to philosophize about ourselves. And compare ourselves with others.</p><p>It's only months later when I was doing The Drill Down with a friend a week later that its usefulness came to me in a flash.</p><p><strong>Everyone's been looking at personality tests all wrong.</strong></p><p>It's always the same thing. You do the test. You get your 4 special letters, and then they go read the description that tells you "what kind of person you are".</p><p>And then one of two things happen after that:</p><p>1. You navel-gaze at how "perfect" the results seem. (I mean, read through most of the comments on the final results page and you'll see what I mean. There are a million "This is so me..." comments down the page.)</p><p>OR</p><p>2. You call B.S. on these kinds of tests. (Usually me.) You feel that everyone is "both letters", or has been "both letters" at different points. So who cares?</p><p>(Hell, I can be both polarities within one day! So I agree.)</p><p>But both ways of looking at the results are missing the point.</p><p>Let me show you what I mean.</p><p>First. Go take the test yourself right now.</p><p>What I'm about to show you is only powerful if you can measure it against the results you get for yourself.</p><p>I understand your skepticism.</p><p>But humor me. It'll be worth it.</p><p>This link will open another tab:</p><p><a href="https://www.16personalities.com/free-personality-test" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">MEYERS-BRIGGS PERSONALITY TEST</a></p><p><br></p><p>===</p><p><br></p><p>Okay, so you have your 4 "letters", right?</p><p>If you answered everything truthfully, it should be a pretty accurate snapshot of you at this moment in time.</p><p>Now do this:</p><p>Write out all the opposite letters that you are NOT.</p><p>So, if you're an INTJ for example, your new letters would become ESFP.</p><p>And then... Write out the full extent of what those opposite letters mean. Here's the full guide:</p><p><br></p><h3>Where you get your energy:</h3><h3>I - Introverted: quiet, reserved</h3><h3>E - Extroverted: loud, outgoing</h3><h3><br></h3><h3>How your day unfolds:</h3><h3>N - Intuitive: no rules, you trust your gut, love randomness</h3><h3>S - Structure: predictability, love planning, on-time, you love rules</h3><h3><br></h3><h3>How you make decisions:</h3><h3>F - Feeling: "in your body", and emotional decision making</h3><h3>T - Thinking: logical and rational decision making</h3><h3><br></h3><h3>This is how you approach problems:</h3><h3>P - Perceiving: Indecisive, like to keep options open</h3><h3>J - Judging: Decisive, things are black and white</h3><p><br></p><p>Now stare at those "opposite" letters on the page... And then come to this understanding: </p><h1>The Meyers-Briggs test isn't about discovering "who you are". No... Instead, it's a way for you to discover the polar opposite character traits that you need to adopt to achieve who you want to become.</h1><p>Let that sink in.</p><p>I know you're gonna want to fight me on this one. People always do.</p><p>But, you'll come to realize that it's the only way forward.</p><p>In psychology lingo, that opposite personality has a name.</p><p>It's called 'The Shadow Self'.</p><p>And you need to embrace your shadow self if you want to get to your next level.</p><p>You know when people say "get out of your comfort zone"? Bungee jumping or "swimming with sharks" is not what they should be engaging in.</p><p>FACING YOUR SHADOW SELF shows you the real departure you need to make to escape your comfort zone. It's so blatant and clear, and even an overthinker can't fool themselves out of this one.</p><h2>THE PROCESS: You look at your problem. And then you try to see which of your opposite letters (or combination of) will help you solve that problem.</h2><p>So, for example...</p><p>If you're an introvert-- Maybe you need to put yourself in more situations where you'll be in the spotlight more.</p><p>Or if you're an extrovert, you need to shut the fuck up. You need to listen more... Be quiet more. Move away from being the center of attention for a little while. </p><p>Embracing the shadow self will make you feel like you're not "being you".</p><p>Your friends and family will ask: who kidnapped the "real you".</p><p>Don't panic.</p><p>Wait for the <a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/12084289" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">secondary effects</a>. </p><p>This is the pathway to your evolution playing out: </p><p>You're beginning to master your polarities.</p><p>I'm from the school of thought that believes that people can change at any point in their lives. People don't have to be victims of their current limitations.</p><p>I mean, when I look around me I see people that have bigger setbacks than I do and they're THRIVING despite any of them.</p><p>So my controversial thought for you today is: </p><p>Any problems that you're having right now... In any aspect of your life... Maybe it's just because you're not pivoting enough towards one of your other polarities when you ought to be.</p><p><em>Maybe you have a "shadow self" that could use some leaning into.</em></p><p>You don't have to embrace all of your opposites at the same time. It could be one or two of 'the opposite letters' that you need to conquer to achieve your current goals.</p><p>Try it.</p><p>Think about a struggle you're going through. A relationship problem, or some "work thing", or another issue that keeps you up at night...</p><p>Look at your shadow letters again. If you learned to pivot more towards that extreme, is there one of them that might make your situation better?</p><p>Are you in a rocky relationship right now? Maybe you rely too much on feelings (F) when you make decisions about your significant other? Or perhaps you rely too much on logic (L)?</p><p>Are you trying to take things to the next level with your photography? Let me guess. You always use intuition and a "no rules" attitude (N) to guide your decisions? Maybe what you need to evolve and get out of your rut is structure (S)?</p><p>Or whatever... Imagine the exact opposite. Get it?</p><p>People love examples. So here's another one: Is life boring right now? (Damn, really kid?) If you were more EXTROVERTED, could it add some spice? Or maybe if you were more INTROVERTED you might discover exciting details within... Or notice things around you that you've always missed because you were taking up so much room?</p><p>Or have you been the 50/50 type for your whole life? Maybe you need to hang out at either one of the extremes to get a few new wins?</p><p>These crazy new results is what the world looks like when you learn how to embrace the shadow self.</p><p>I remember when I was starting out as a photographer... I knew I couldn't act like an introvert if I was gonna get the kind of pictures that I wanted to get. So I embraced my opposite polarity. I created a character that was outrageously outgoing for those situations.</p><p>Remember that Seinfeld episode called 'The Opposite'? Where George Constanza behaves the opposite way of how he usually does to see how much his life would change?</p><p>And from that moment on his loser character becomes a winner.</p><p>Well, that classic episode was about George embracing his shadow self. </p><p>It doesn't mean you can't have your favorite way of being whenever you're chilling with your family. But I'm just saying, when you plateau on your goals... Now you know what to do. <strong>Do the opposite for a while. </strong>Or even embrace it for a lifetime like I did with my own "situational extroversion". </p><p>It's up to you.</p><p>What a mindfuck right?</p><p>Every goal that you're having trouble reaching right now... Is easily reached by your shadow self.</p><p>It's almost annoying.</p><p>Until you realized that your shadow self IS LAYING DORMANT INSIDE YOU.</p><p>The shadow self is where all your untapped potential lies.</p><p>It'll feel scary to go down these new paths.</p><p>You will try to grasp for every excuse you can find to stay the same. You might already be doing it right now, reading this.</p><p>And to make matters worse...</p><p>You'll rationalize that some of the people you hate on the most-- They naturally have the qualities of the shadow self you've for so long rejected. </p><p>THAT'S THE POINT!</p><p>It kills you, knowing that your best path forward is adopting some of the same characteristics from those you reject to achieve your dreams.</p><p>Like... You hate that boring girl that plans everything (S). You're proud that you're seen as a free spirit (N). Well, maybe it's time to be more of "the planning type" to... get your music career going or whatever. Maybe there's something you can learn from them.</p><p>Or maybe it's the opposite. Maybe you need to be more of a free spirit to let more magic happen around you. Learn from someone who is by modeling their behavior.</p><p>The point is: Let go of all the biases you're holding onto.</p><p>Start embracing your nemesis' best qualities. Make those qualities part of YOUR REPERTOIRE </p><p>That negative chatter stopping you from embracing your shadow is just your ego talking.</p><p>It wants you to resist change.</p><p>Even society wants you to suppress your shadow side. It much prefers you to stay predictable and boring.</p><p>Fight society's desire for you to conform... You're an artist, remember?</p><p>Fight your ego's desire to be consistent. Start joyfully leaning into your polar opposite.</p><p>Swing your pendulum to the other side.</p><p>Here is a list of polarities beyond Meyers-Briggs. Which opposite polarity could help you out right now?</p><p><br></p><h3>Logic | Intuition</h3><h3>Tight with money | Loose with money</h3><h3>Masculine | Feminine</h3><h3>Disciplined | Undisciplined</h3><h3>Sensible | Silly</h3><h3>Organized | Disorganized</h3><h3>Shy | Outgoing</h3><h3>Artistic and creative | Logical and precise</h3><h3>Nature | Polluted City</h3><h3>Playful | Serious</h3><h3>Healthy | Unhealthy</h3><h3>Early Riser | Night Owl</h3><h3>Science | Religion</h3><h3>Meditation and Spirituality | Money and Cigars</h3><h3>Giving to others | Selfish to myself</h3><h3>Think more about the past | Think more about the future</h3><h3>Charity | Greed</h3><h3>Loud | Quiet</h3><h3>Bold and well presented | Understated</h3><p><br></p><p>There is no one true you.</p><p>Who you are right now is merely the result of choices you made in the past. Like what your favorite ice cream is. Or, if you even like ice cream in the first place. </p><p>But now you know: When what's in the light isn't working, embrace what's in the dark. </p><p>Someone (who doesn't have your best interest in mind) convinced you that people can't change. And fooled you into thinking that "being balanced" is good. And that this thinking will somehow lead you to "happiness". It doesn't. It leads to normal. If "normal" makes you happy, then all the power to you. But if you're reaching for "amazing", "next level", or "extraordinary"… The only option left is living in and embracing the extremes. </p><p>Start integrating your shadow self into all the aspects of your life that you want to improve. Tap into it's full power. </p><p>Start today.</p><p><em>The 21st century belongs to those who master their polarities. </em></p><p><em>P.S. You want another way of looking at it? When you're struggling with things, you ARE your shadow self. And your opposite traits are the light you need to move into to evolve. Either way you look at it... The process is gonna hurt. But that's how transformations towards legendary always begin.</em></p><p></p>
</div>

    
  </div>

      <div data-original-comment-body="This is just mind-blowing.

Mine is ENTJ, an ambitious go-getter kind of personality that some people crave for(or boast about) because such MBTI memes portray them as "true leaders". Preposterous enough, as I'm going through hard identity crisis and not being able to even care about myself. Meanwhile here are some of the advice that my shadow ISFP gave me...

- Get your ass in your place and reserve your energy for the sake of your emotional well being.
- Please shut the f**k up and keep your voice low. (probably the hardest proverb to swallow)
- Learn how to not give a s**t to some issues. Just leave it as it is.
- Likewise, stop being a leader in every communities. You don't have to fix all things that you see.
- Appreciate little moments in your everyday surroundings.
- Even if you don't have any plans you'll end up somewhere great and it's totally up to your mind. Learn how to improvise and let go of yourself sometimes.
- Do some little cute tabletop art, be it a freehand sketch of cats or a short poem from your feelings. Don't try to be perfect, though.
- Focus on the present. Not your past, not your future ambitions.
- Rather than to achieve high standard that society tells you to, it's okay to fail and stay mediocre sometimes.
- Be nice to people. Please.

Funny thing is that my mom is ISFP-ish and that was what irritated me whenever I was in a trip with her(and vice versa). However incorporating my mom-like shadow self greatly relieved my anxiety. This is just amazing." class="comments__block-box__body">This is just mind-blowing.

- Get your ass in your place and reserve your energy for the sake of your emotional well being.
- Please shut the f**k up and keep your voice low. (probably the hardest proverb to swallow)
- Learn how to not give a s**t to some issues. Just leave it as it is.
- Likewise, stop being a leader in every communities. You don't have to fix all things that you see.
- Appreciate little moments in your everyday surroundings.
- Even if you don't have any plans you'll end up somewhere great and it's totally up to your mind. Learn how to improvise and let go of yourself sometimes.
- Do some little cute tabletop art, be it a freehand sketch of cats or a short poem from your feelings. Don't try to be perfect, though.
- Focus on the present. Not your past, not your future ambitions.
- Rather than to achieve high standard that society tells you to, it's okay to fail and stay mediocre sometimes.
- Be nice to people. Please.

Funny thing is that my mom is ISFP-ish and that was what irritated me whenever I was in a trip with her(and vice versa). However incorporating my mom-like shadow self greatly relieved my anxiety. This is just amazing.</div>

      <div data-original-comment-body="Very interesting 🤯
For me: i hate people wearing 'masks', hiding parts of themselves. It's even my message! 'Unmask. Be real. Express how you feel'. 

But the truth is, I've been feeling for a couple of weeks now, that unmasking is not always the way. There's a lot of strength and selfcare in NOT being completely vulnerable. But ive been avoiding this truth cause it is contradictory with my message 

So i think two things: 
1. My message is more nuanced than I thought it was. Maybe Ive outgrown it partially and I need to update it? 

2. It would be helpful for me to be partially masked in my business. Yes, my message is about unmasking and authenticity, but for me as an artist and businessowner: there's a time and place for it. 

I need less Feeling (acting impulsively by sharing vulnerable) and more Thinking (carefully dosing my vulnerable shares. In the end, my business isn't about me, eventhough I can practixe what I preach (unmask), in the end it's always about my audience. And they are not my therapist). 

Plus I need to be less fucking serious with my business/marketing endeavors. Yes I need to keep doing them, but I need to relax a little. I need to stop squeezing in it all the time. It need to give it space and air.

Having an epiphany right now: ive been squeezing so hard, because in my mind im focussed on my 'negative outcome' list. I need to invest in the 'positive outcome' list. I'll write it right now during my morning ritual.

Thanks Bronques 🧡💪🏻🫶🏻

So i think two things: 
1. My message is more nuanced than I thought it was. Maybe Ive outgrown it partially and I need to update it? 

2. It would be helpful for me to be partially masked in my business. Yes, my message is about unmasking and authenticity, but for me as an artist and businessowner: there's a time and place for it.

Plus I need to be less fucking serious with my business/marketing endeavors. Yes I need to keep doing them, but I need to relax a little. I need to stop squeezing in it all the time. It need to give it space and air.

Thanks Bronques 🧡💪🏻🫶🏻</div>
